blockers.title: Admin member dossier leaks member email through audit actors without audited contact reveal
blockers.evidence: Spec F14 requires contact details to stay behind an audited per-member reveal. `convex/admin/members.ts` returns `recent_audit.actor` directly in the admin dossier, and `src/admin/views/MemberDetail.tsx` renders it as `by {row.actor}`. Several member actions write the member email as `actor`, including `convex/members.ts` profile updates, `convex/events.ts` RSVPs, `convex/opportunities.ts` applications, and `convex/lib/certificates.ts` certificate issuance. Opening a dossier can therefore expose the member's raw email without calling `revealMemberContact` and without creating the required reveal audit row.
blockers.required_fix: Do not return or render raw member email actors in `getMemberAdmin` recent audit rows. Mask them or map member-owned actions to a non-PII label while keeping full immutable audit data internally. Add a regression test that seeds an audit row with `actor` equal to the member email and proves the admin dossier JSON/UI does not expose it before `revealMemberContact`.

blockers.title: Live event date/time can be changed silently through `upsertEvent`, bypassing postpone notifications
blockers.evidence: Spec A3 requires postponed events to use a confirm path and notify every registered member in-app. The UI disables live date/time editing and tells admins to use Postpone because editing would move the event silently, but `convex/admin/events.ts` does not enforce this server-side. `upsertEvent` accepts and patches `starts_at`/`ends_at` for published or postponed events, writes only an `upsertEvent` audit row, and sends no notifications. The notifying path exists only in `postponeEvent`, so direct Convex calls or future admin code can bypass the safeguard.
blockers.required_fix: Enforce the live-event date/time lock in `upsertEvent`: reject `starts_at` or `ends_at` changes once an event is non-draft, or route those changes through the same `postponeEvent` implementation that writes the postpone audit and notifies all live bookings. Add tests proving `upsertEvent` cannot silently move a live event and `postponeEvent` still notifies registrants.

non_blocking_notes: `git diff --check main...HEAD` passed, aside from sandbox `xcrun` cache warnings.
`npm test` and `npm run check` could not complete in the read-only sandbox because Vitest/Astro attempted to write generated files under `node_modules/.vite-temp` and `.astro`.
No real secrets were found in the reviewed diff. No member-facing em dashes were found in added portal/admin strings; em dashes remain only in docs/comments.

spec_compliance.criterion: F14 - admin member dossier masks contact details and contact reveal is explicit and audited
spec_compliance.evidence: Direct contact fields are masked, but `recent_audit.actor` can contain the member email and is rendered before any audited reveal.

spec_compliance.criterion: A3 - cancel/postpone flows notify registered members and cannot move events silently
spec_compliance.evidence: `postponeEvent` notifies registrants, but `upsertEvent` can still patch live event date/time without notifications.

spec_compliance.criterion: A1-A4 - member event discovery, eligibility, priority windows, RSVP/waitlist basics
spec_compliance.evidence: Reviewed server-side lane gates, active lifecycle checks, priority-window checks, waitlist promotion capacity checks, and no `restricted_unknown` event visibility.

spec_compliance.criterion: B5-B8 - opportunity listing, application, admin decisioning, and winner eligibility
spec_compliance.evidence: Reviewed lane/audience gating, profile-complete checks, duplicate-application prevention, audience locks once open, and winner revalidation at decision time.

spec_compliance.criterion: D11 - directory hides minors, restricted_unknown members, and private contact fields
spec_compliance.evidence: Directory queries require active non-minor/non-restricted viewers and listed members, and returned rows omit email, mobile, gender, DOB, and bio.

spec_compliance.criterion: H18 - reports use aggregate/count views rather than bulk row export
spec_compliance.evidence: Reports view uses aggregate query endpoints for portal, event, opportunity, and certificate counts.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks - member-facing copy uses plain language and no em dashes
spec_compliance.evidence: Search of changed member-facing portal/admin strings found no em dashes or obvious placeholder member-facing copy.

spec_compliance.criterion: J21 - full automated verification evidence is available
spec_compliance.evidence: Typecheck passed, but full test/check verification could not run in this read-only review sandbox because the tools attempted to write generated/cache files.

summary: Do not ship. The slice has two concrete security/product blockers: audited contact reveal is bypassed by raw audit actors in the member dossier, and live events can be moved silently through the backend `upsertEvent` path without registrant notifications.
